_meta.description: S4 inference proposals — HYPOTHESES, sourcing-gated. NEVER auto-promoted; review + source + promote by hand.

sibc.entity: Advances against Fixed Deposits (Including FCNR (B), NRNR Deposits etc.)
Weaker Sections including net PSLC- SF/MF

proposals.label: UPI incentive shift → QR code deployment economics
proposals.mechanism: Changes in NPCI or government cashback/MDR subsidy schemes for UPI versus QR codes alter merchant incentive to maintain Bharat QR infrastructure
proposals.required_source: NPCI circulars on UPI incentive schemes or Ministry of Electronics and IT notifications on digital payment promotion budgets

verification.source_title: Cabinet approves Incentive scheme for promotion of low-value BHIM-UPI transactions (P2M)
verification.excerpt: incentive scheme for promotion of low-value BHIM-UPI transactions (P2M) will be implemented at an estimated outlay of 1,500 crore
verification.note: Found government incentive schemes for UPI (₹1,500 crore FY 2024-25) but no primary source documenting separate/differential incentive schemes for Bharat QR infrastructure that would create the claimed merchant incentive differential between UPI and QR code acceptance.

proposals.label: Business correspondent regulation → micro ATM viability
proposals.mechanism: RBI revisions to business correspondent licensing, cash-handling limits, or commission structures change the economics of deploying micro ATMs in rural areas
proposals.required_source: RBI Master Direction on Business Correspondents or RBI notifications on cash withdrawal limits at BC points

verification.note: No standalone RBI Master Direction on Business Correspondents found. BC provisions appear scattered across KYC Master Direction and older circulars. Found references to BC commission discussions by DFS (Jan 2025) and general AePS limits, but no authoritative RBI source directly linking BC regulation revisions to micro ATM viability economics as claimed.

proposals.label: ATM interchange fee revision → on-site versus off-site economics
proposals.mechanism: RBI changes to interchange fees for on-us versus off-us ATM transactions shift bank incentives to prioritize on-site (own-customer) ATM deployment over shared networks
proposals.required_source: RBI circular on ATM interchange fee structure or Payment and Settlement Systems Act notifications

verification.excerpt: The card issuing bank pays an interchange fee to the operator of the ATM when a customer makes a transaction at an ATM that does not belong to the card-issuing bank
verification.note: The claimed mechanism is contradicted by how ATM interchange fees actually work. Interchange fees apply only to off-us (other bank) transactions, not on-us (own bank) transactions. Banks EARN interchange fees when other banks' customers use their ATMs, creating an incentive to participate in shared networks, not avoid them. The RBI circular increases fees for off-us transactions but does not create an incentive to prioritize own-customer ATMs over shared networks as claimed.

proposals.label: Credit card cash advance fee cap → ATM withdrawal demand
proposals.mechanism: RBI or issuer policy changes capping fees or interest rates on credit card cash advances reduce the cost, shifting volume away from ATM withdrawals
proposals.required_source: RBI Master Direction on Credit Card and Debit Card operations or issuer bank tariff schedule changes

verification.note: RBI Master Direction on Credit Card and Debit Card (2022) mandates disclosure of cash advance fees but does NOT cap them. Late payment fees were capped (Oct 2024), but cash advance fees (2.5-3.5%) remain market-determined. No RBI policy reducing cash advance costs was found.

proposals.label: E-commerce TCS threshold → online card spend
proposals.mechanism: CBDT Tax Collected at Source rules on e-commerce transactions above certain thresholds deter high-value online purchases, reducing credit card e-commerce value
proposals.required_source: CBDT notifications on TCS under Section 206C(1H) of Income Tax Act or Finance Act amendments

verification.source_title: Budget 2025: TCS on Sale of Goods to be Omitted from April 2025 - TaxGuru
verification.excerpt: Section 206C(1H) requires any person being a seller who receives consideration for sale of any goods exceeding Rs 50 lakhs to collect tax from the buyer at 0.1%
verification.note: Section 206C(1H) applied to B2B transactions (seller turnover >Rs.10 crore, buyer purchases >Rs.50 lakh), not consumer e-commerce. It was omitted April 1, 2025. No evidence supports the claimed mechanism that it deterred online consumer purchases or reduced credit card e-commerce value. The provision was designed for high-value business transactions, not retail credit card spending.

proposals.label: Contactless payment limit increase → POS transaction mix
proposals.mechanism: RBI raising the per-transaction limit for contactless card payments encourages higher-value POS usage while cannibalizing e-commerce for mid-ticket purchases
proposals.required_source: RBI circular on contactless card transaction limits or Payment and Settlement Systems notifications

verification.source_title: RBI Circular DPSS.CO.PD No.752/02.14.003/2020-21 dated December 04, 2020
verification.excerpt: per transaction limit to ₹ 5,000/- for card transactions in contactless mode at Points of Sale (PoS) terminals
verification.note: RBI did raise contactless limit from Rs 2,000 to Rs 5,000 in Dec 2020, but the circular states the goal as boosting digital payment adoption and customer convenience. No mention of encouraging higher-value POS usage or cannibalizing e-commerce for mid-ticket purchases. The mechanism about e-commerce cannibalization is not supported by the source.

proposals.label: Fuel surcharge waiver removal → debit card POS demand
proposals.mechanism: Banks discontinuing fuel surcharge waivers on debit cards shift consumer preference to credit cards or UPI for fuel purchases, reducing debit POS volume
proposals.required_source: Public tariff schedules from major PSU and private banks or Petroleum Ministry pricing notifications

verification.note: No authoritative primary source (RBI, NPCI, Ministry of Finance, or PSU bank official policy) found documenting systematic discontinuation of fuel surcharge waivers on debit cards. Fuel surcharge waivers on debit cards are voluntary bank-specific benefits that vary by institution and card tier, not regulatory mandates. While UPI structurally avoids fuel surcharges (making it more attractive), this is due to different payment economics rather than removal of debit card benefits. The claimed causal mechanism - that banks are discontinuing debit card fuel waivers and this shifts consumers to credit cards/UPI - lacks verifiable documentation from requested authoritative sources.

proposals.label: RuPay cashback scheme → debit e-commerce uptake
proposals.mechanism: NPCI or government cashback schemes specifically targeting RuPay debit cards for e-commerce transactions boost debit card online spending
proposals.required_source: NPCI announcements on RuPay promotional schemes or Ministry of Finance notifications on digital payment incentives

verification.source_title: Cabinet approves the incentive scheme for promotion of RuPay Debit Cards and low-value BHIM-UPI transactions (P2M) - Press Information Bureau
verification.excerpt: acquiring banks will be provided financial incentive, for promoting Point-of-Sale (PoS) and e-commerce transactions using RuPay Debit Cards
verification.note: Government incentive schemes launched from FY 2021-22 onwards explicitly provide financial incentives to acquiring banks for promoting e-commerce transactions using RuPay Debit Cards. The Ministry of Finance guidelines specify 0.40% incentive rate (capped at ₹100) for RuPay Debit Card e-commerce transactions. Multiple Cabinet approvals (₹2,600 crore for FY 2022-23, ₹3,500 crore for FY 2023-24) confirm government support targeting RuPay debit card online spending.

proposals.source: Cabinet approves the incentive scheme for promotion of RuPay Debit Cards and low-value BHIM-UPI transactions (P2M) - Press Information Bureau
proposals.source_excerpt: acquiring banks will be provided financial incentive, for promoting Point-of-Sale (PoS) and e-commerce transactions using RuPay Debit Cards

proposals.label: Cash-at-POS regulatory clarity → debit withdrawal behavior
proposals.mechanism: RBI clarification or guidelines on cash withdrawal at POS terminals provide merchants and banks regulatory certainty, expanding this service and shifting withdrawal patterns
proposals.required_source: RBI circular on cash withdrawal at Point of Sale or Payment Systems Vision documents

verification.source_title: RBI FAQ - Cash Withdrawal Facility at Point of Sale (PoS) Terminals
verification.excerpt: cardholders can withdraw cash using their debit cards and full KYC prepaid cards issued by banks
verification.note: RBI issued circular DPSS.CO.PD.No.147/02.14.003/2009-10 on July 22, 2009 permitting cash withdrawals at POS terminals, providing regulatory clarity that allowed banks (with board approval) to expand this service. Multiple subsequent circulars (2013, 2015, 2019, 2020, 2021) provided further clarifications on limits, eligible cards, and merchant requirements. This regulatory framework enabled the facility to expand, shifting debit card withdrawal patterns from ATM-only to include merchant POS terminals.

proposals.source: RBI FAQ - Cash Withdrawal Facility at Point of Sale (PoS) Terminals
proposals.source_excerpt: cardholders can withdraw cash using their debit cards and full KYC prepaid cards issued by banks

proposals.label: ATM cash replenishment cost → on-us withdrawal economics
proposals.mechanism: Rising currency logistics costs or RBI cash supply constraints make banks discourage off-us ATM withdrawals while promoting on-site or POS cash withdrawal
proposals.required_source: RBI currency management reports or logistics cost indices for cash-in-transit services

verification.note: While RBI reports confirm rising ATM cash logistics costs and the RBI has responded by raising ATM interchange fees (to Rs 19 in 2025), no authoritative RBI source was found that specifically establishes a policy mechanism where banks discourage off-us ATM withdrawals while promoting on-site or POS cash withdrawal as a cost management strategy. The RBI's approach has been to adjust fee structures rather than redirect transaction channels.

proposals.label: Tokenization mandate → card transaction classification
proposals.mechanism: RBI tokenization requirements for card-on-file transactions reclassify some previously counted e-commerce volumes into 'other' categories in reporting
proposals.required_source: RBI circular on tokenization of card transactions (dated March 2020 and subsequent updates)

verification.source_title: RBI Circular on Restriction on Storage of Actual Card Data (Card-on-File)
verification.excerpt: no entity in the card transaction / payment chain, other than the card issuers and / or card networks, shall store the CoF data
verification.note: RBI tokenization circulars from March 2020 and subsequent updates focus on data security and storage restrictions. No RBI circular or authoritative source found that documents tokenization requirements causing reclassification of e-commerce transactions into 'other' reporting categories. The circulars address technical implementation of tokenization but do not contain provisions about transaction classification for statistical reporting purposes.

proposals.label: White-label ATM operator exits → micro ATM substitution failure
proposals.mechanism: Specific white-label ATM operators exiting rural markets due to unprofitability create cash access gaps that micro ATMs cannot fill at scale
proposals.required_source: RBI list of authorized White Label ATM Operators or Tata Communications/Vakrangee/BTI Payments annual reports on ATM deployment

verification.note: Searched RBI sources, operator reports, and news. Found evidence of WLA viability challenges (low rural transaction volumes, high costs) and Tata Communications' 2026 divestment of its WLA business to another operator (not a market exit). Found no authoritative source documenting that WLA operator exits created cash access gaps that micro ATMs failed to fill at scale. The claimed mechanism lacks primary source support.

proposals.label: COVID-era contactless habit persistence → card preference shift
proposals.mechanism: Post-pandemic consumer continuation of contactless payment preferences drives sustained POS growth while reducing cash-based ATM and certain e-commerce behaviors
proposals.required_source: RBI Payments Vision reports or NPCI transaction data showing contactless versus chip-and-PIN transaction shares

verification.excerpt: steps taken by Reserve Bank along with additional thrust provided by the pandemic have accelerated the shift, leading to a dramatic increase in contactless and online payments
verification.note: RBI confirms COVID accelerated contactless AND online payments together. No authoritative source found supporting the specific claim that post-pandemic contactless preference reduces e-commerce behaviors. The mechanism's second half (reducing certain e-commerce behaviors) lacks empirical support from RBI/NPCI primary sources.

proposals.label: UPI-credit card linkage → debit card POS cannibalization
proposals.mechanism: NPCI allowing credit cards on UPI diverts transaction volume from debit card POS to UPI QR, creating cross-product substitution
proposals.required_source: NPCI circular on linking RuPay credit cards to UPI (June 2022) and subsequent volume data

verification.source_title: NPCI Operating Circular for RuPay Credit Cards linked to UPI (October 4, 2022)
verification.excerpt: Merchants will benefit from the increase in consumption by being part of the credit ecosystem with acceptance of credit cards using asset lite QR codes
verification.note: The NPCI circular and RBI announcement confirm credit cards were linked to UPI starting June/October 2022, and debit card volumes did decline sharply (65% from 2019-2024). However, authoritative sources (RBI Payment Systems Report, PwC analysis) attribute debit card POS cannibalization primarily to UPI adoption generally—not specifically to credit-card-on-UPI. The debit card decline was already underway before credit cards were added to UPI, suggesting the mechanism conflates general UPI substitution with the specific credit-card linkage feature.

proposals.label: BNPL partner POS integration → credit card POS boost
proposals.mechanism: Buy-Now-Pay-Later platforms partnering with card networks enable BNPL checkout via virtual credit cards at POS, boosting credit POS volume while reducing standalone e-commerce transactions
proposals.required_source: RBI guidelines on prepaid payment instruments and co-branding or fintech-bank partnership announcements

verification.source_title: RBI Governor Das Statement on BNPL and PPIs, July 2022
verification.excerpt: RBI issued a notification disallowing non-bank prepaid payment instruments (PPIs) from loading through credit lines
verification.note: The claimed mechanism is contradicted by RBI policy. In June 2022, RBI explicitly banned non-bank PPIs from being loaded with credit lines, blocking BNPL platforms from using virtual prepaid/credit cards on card networks. RBI ordered BNPL firms to operate through regulated NBFC lending partnerships rather than via card-network POS rails. The search found no RBI, NPCI, or Ministry of Finance documentation supporting the claim that BNPL-card network partnerships boost credit card POS volumes; instead, RBI restricted this exact model. While global BNPL providers do use virtual cards on Visa/Mastercard networks, this structure was prohibited in India for non-bank fintechs. Credit card POS growth documented by RBI (transaction volumes doubled 2019-2024) stems from traditional credit card usage, UPI-credit integration, and bank-issued EMI products—not from BNPL virtual card POS integration.

proposals.label: Credit-card revolving vs transactor ratio → interchange-fee revenue
proposals.mechanism: Higher revolving balances reduce transaction volumes per cardholder, lowering aggregate interchange revenue captured in SIBC Statement1/4.5 fee-income flows
proposals.required_source: RBI Master Direction on Issuance and Operation of Prepaid Payment Instruments / Master Circular on Credit Card Operations for interchange-fee schedules and transaction-reporting requirements

verification.note: RBI Master Direction on Credit Card and Debit Card – Issuance and Conduct Directions, 2022 exists but does not contain interchange fee schedules or the claimed revolving-to-transactor mechanism. No regulatory document 'SIBC Statement1/4.5' was found in RBI's reporting framework. The claimed causal link between revolving balances, transaction volume, and interchange revenue is not supported by any identifiable RBI primary source.

proposals.label: Co-branded card partnership share → spend-channel mix
proposals.mechanism: Co-branded cards with specific merchant partners (airlines, e-commerce) concentrate spend in ecom/other categories rather than POS, shifting transaction-value distribution
proposals.required_source: RBI Master Direction - Credit Card and Debit Card – Issuance and Conduct for co-branding disclosure rules, and NPCI quarterly payment-system statistics for channel breakdowns

verification.source_title: RBI Master Direction – Credit Card and Debit Card – Issuance and Conduct Directions, 2022
verification.excerpt: co-branding arrangement shall be as per the Board approved policy of the card-issuer
verification.note: RBI Master Direction addresses co-branding disclosure rules, transparency, and roles of partners but does not document the claimed mechanism about spend-channel concentration (e-commerce vs POS). NPCI retail payment statistics do not provide channel-level breakdowns by co-branded vs regular cards. The specific causal mechanism linking co-branded partnerships to transaction-value distribution shifts across channels is not supported by primary regulatory or NPCI sources.

proposals.label: UPI credit-line linking → card-withdrawal substitution
proposals.mechanism: RBI allowing credit lines on UPI reduces ATM withdrawal volumes as users access credit via UPI instead of physical card cash advances
proposals.required_source: RBI circular DBR.No.Leg.BC.98/09.07.005/2022-23 dated June 8, 2023 on linking credit lines to UPI, and NPCI UPI transaction statistics

verification.source_title: RBI Circular RBI/2023-24/58 - Operation of Pre-Sanctioned Credit Lines at Banks through UPI
verification.excerpt: payments through a pre-sanctioned credit line issued by a Scheduled Commercial Bank to individuals, with prior consent
verification.note: The RBI circular CO.DPSS.POLC.No.S-567/02-23-001/2023-2024 dated September 4, 2023 allows credit lines to be linked to UPI, but it does not mention or establish any causal mechanism whereby this reduces ATM withdrawal volumes or substitutes for credit card cash advances. The circular only enables credit lines as a funding account for UPI transactions. The claimed DBR.No.Leg.BC.98/09.07.005/2022-23 reference does not exist in RBI records.

proposals.label: Rental-payment acceptance → other-txn-value concentration
proposals.mechanism: Property-rental payment acceptance via credit cards creates large-ticket transactions categorised as 'other' rather than POS or ecom, skewing value concentrations
proposals.required_source: RBI Master Direction on Credit Card Operations for MCC classification rules, and payment-gateway reporting on rental-category transaction growth

verification.note: No authoritative RBI or NPCI source found that explicitly documents rental payment MCC classification rules causing 'other' category transaction value concentration. While MCC codes 6513/7349 are used for rentals by some banks, and RBI Master Directions exist, no primary regulatory document supports the specific mechanism that rental payments are categorized as 'other' (versus POS/ecom) in official reporting frameworks.

proposals.label: Add-on card issuance intensity → spend-volume without proportional fee-base
proposals.mechanism: Banks issuing multiple add-on cards per primary account inflate transaction volumes without corresponding annual-fee revenue in SIBC fee-income
proposals.required_source: RBI Master Direction - Credit Card and Debit Card – Issuance and Conduct for add-on card reporting requirements, and bank annual reports for primary vs add-on card ratios

verification.source_title: RBI Master Direction – Credit Card and Debit Card – Issuance and Conduct Directions, 2022
verification.excerpt: The add-on cards shall be issued only to the persons specifically identified by the principal cardholder
verification.note: RBI Master Direction addresses add-on card issuance rules and liability but contains no specific reporting requirements distinguishing primary vs add-on card transaction volumes or annual fee revenue allocation. The claimed mechanism that add-on cards inflate spend volumes without proportional fee revenue in SIBC reporting is not supported by this regulatory source. No evidence found that RBI mandates separate reporting of primary vs add-on card metrics that would create this distortion.

proposals.label: Fuel-surcharge waiver withdrawal → POS-txn decline
proposals.mechanism: Removal of fuel-surcharge waivers by issuers reduces fuel-station POS transactions, shifting spend to other categories or non-card payment
proposals.required_source: Petroleum Planning and Analysis Cell (PPAC) fuel-price data series, and issuer-bank quarterly disclosures on card-benefit changes

verification.note: Extensive search found no authoritative primary source (RBI, NPCI, PPAC, Ministry of Finance, or issuer-bank quarterly disclosures) documenting the claimed causal mechanism. While fuel surcharge waivers are a known credit card feature, and some banks have modified card benefits in 2024, no official data source establishes that waiver withdrawal caused measurable decline in fuel-station POS transactions or shifts to other payment modes. PPAC publishes fuel price data but not card-transaction patterns. Bank disclosures found were promotional materials, not empirical quarterly reports on transaction-volume changes linked to benefit modifications.

proposals.label: EMI-conversion promotional intensity → revolving-balance stock disconnect
proposals.mechanism: Aggressive EMI-conversion offers lock spend into instalment plans reported as term assets rather than revolving credit, decoupling transaction flows from SIBC revolving-credit stock
proposals.required_source: RBI instructions on Fair Practices Code for Credit Card Operations regarding EMI disclosure, and bank quarterly earnings calls for EMI-book reporting

verification.source_title: RBI Master Direction – Credit Card and Debit Card – Issuance and Conduct, 2022
verification.excerpt: EMI programmes on credit cards are governed specifically by Master Direction on Credit Card and Debit Card
verification.note: Found RBI Master Direction governing EMI disclosure requirements and NPCI documentation stating EMIs are 'termed as loan,' but no primary RBI source explicitly confirms that EMI conversions are reported as term assets rather than revolving credit stock in SIBC or other regulatory reporting frameworks. The claimed mechanism about decoupling transaction flows from revolving-credit stock lacks direct RBI documentation.

proposals.label: Merchant-category restricted cards → spend-channel ceilings
proposals.mechanism: Corporate and commercial cards with MCC restrictions cap ecom or international spend, creating structural ceilings in specific transaction categories
proposals.required_source: RBI Master Circular on Credit Card Operations for corporate-card rules, and NPCI payment-system indicator reports for commercial-card transaction shares

verification.note: RBI Master Directions permit corporate/business credit cards but do not mandate MCC-based spend restrictions or category-specific ceilings. MCC controls are voluntary corporate expense management tools per ISO 18245, not regulatory requirements. NPCI does not publish commercial-card transaction share data by MCC category. The claimed mechanism conflates voluntary issuer/corporate policies with regulatory mandates.

proposals.label: October 2022 RBI unsecured-lending risk-weight hike → card-limit reductions
proposals.mechanism: RBI increased risk weights on unsecured credit in November 2023, causing issuers to reduce card limits and suppress ATM-withdrawal and high-value POS transaction capacity
proposals.required_source: RBI Circular DOR.STR.REC.68/21.06.201/2023-24 dated November 16, 2023 on enhancement of risk weights for consumer credit exposure

verification.excerpt: risk weights on such exposures of SCBs by 25 percentage points
verification.note: RBI circular increased risk weights on unsecured credit and credit card receivables on Nov 16, 2023 (not Oct 2022). Correct circular number is DOR.STR.REC.57 (not 68). While risk weight increases were implemented, no authoritative source documents the claimed causal mechanism of card limit reductions or suppression of ATM/POS transaction capacity as direct consequences.

proposals.label: Tokenisation mandate → ecom-transaction settlement lags
proposals.mechanism: Card-on-file tokenisation requirements create settlement and reconciliation delays, temporarily distorting ecom transaction-value timing in monthly flows
proposals.required_source: RBI notification on Card-on-File Tokenisation dated September 7, 2021 and NPCI guidelines on token-transaction reporting standards

verification.source_title: RBI notification on Card-on-File Tokenisation dated September 7, 2021
verification.excerpt: For transaction tracking and/or reconciliation purposes, entities can store limited data – last four digits of actual card number and card issuer's name
verification.note: The RBI notification dated September 7, 2021 on Card-on-File Tokenisation has been confirmed to exist and addresses reconciliation. However, the specific causal mechanism claimed—that tokenisation requirements CREATE settlement and reconciliation DELAYS that temporarily DISTORT ecommerce transaction-value TIMING in monthly flows—is not substantiated in any primary source. The RBI notification permits limited data storage (last 4 digits, issuer name) specifically FOR reconciliation purposes, suggesting accommodation rather than delay creation. No RBI or NPCI document found establishes that the tokenisation mandate caused settlement timing distortions or lags in transaction value flows. The mechanism as stated is not supported by the cited or any available primary regulatory source.

proposals.label: Cross-border transaction corridor shifts → other-txn volatility
proposals.mechanism: Remittance and international-payment corridor mix changes affect 'other transaction' category volumes as cross-border non-POS non-ecom usage fluctuates
proposals.required_source: RBI Liberalised Remittance Scheme statistics and NPCI international transaction data under RuPay

verification.note: RBI publishes LRS remittance statistics and NPCI reports RuPay/UPI international transaction acceptance, but no authoritative source was found documenting a credit card 'other transaction' category or establishing how remittance corridor shifts affect credit card transaction category volatility. RBI credit card statistics are reported as POS and e-commerce categories, not as 'other transactions' affected by cross-border non-POS non-ecom usage.

proposals.label: Contactless-transaction ceiling → ticket-size distribution
proposals.mechanism: RBI contactless-transaction limit changes shift POS transaction distribution between small-ticket tap and larger chip-PIN transactions
proposals.required_source: RBI circular on Enhancement of Limit for Contactless Card Transactions dated January 9, 2021, and subsequent amendments

verification.source_title: RBI Circular DPSS.CO.PD No.752/02.14.003/2020-21 dated December 4, 2020 - Card transactions in Contactless mode
verification.excerpt: Additional Factor of Authentication (AFA) requirement was relaxed for values up to ₹ 2,000/- per transaction for card transactions in contactless mode at Points of Sale (PoS) terminals. It has been decided to increase the per transaction limit to ₹ 5,000/-
verification.note: The RBI circular dated December 4, 2020 (effective January 1, 2021) increased the contactless card transaction limit from Rs 2,000 to Rs 5,000. The circular explicitly states that transactions beyond this limit require Additional Factor of Authentication (PIN) and must be processed as contact payments (chip-PIN). This directly supports the claimed mechanism that RBI contactless-transaction limit changes shift POS transaction distribution between small-ticket tap (no PIN, up to limit) and larger chip-PIN transactions (above limit, requires PIN). The circular affects both credit and debit cards at POS terminals.

proposals.source: RBI Circular DPSS.CO.PD No.752/02.14.003/2020-21 dated December 4, 2020 - Card transactions in Contactless mode
proposals.source_excerpt: Additional Factor of Authentication (AFA) requirement was relaxed for values up to ₹ 2,000/- per transaction for card transactions in contactless mode at Points of Sale (PoS) terminals. It has been decided to increase the per transaction limit to ₹ 5,000/-
